title: README -> START_HERE -> docs route repair
reasoning: Fresh audits showed flat Codeberg adoption and docs-quality route drift. The highest-leverage safe action available right now was to tighten the repo's first-click conversion route so inbound traffic gets the canonical product story and a clearer first-use path.
expected_outcome: Increase the chance that Codeberg-bound evaluators understand the product faster, choose a real first task, and convert into stars, watches, issues, or deeper repo inspection.

measurement_window.success_metric: Any improvement in Codeberg engagement signals or stronger evidence that inbound evaluators reach first-task pages and stay on the repo path.
measurement_window.kill_condition: If adoption remains flat and no stronger inspection/use signals appear after the next window, treat docs-route repair as insufficient by itself and shift effort back to new external distribution/backlink lanes.

verification.method: targeted diff review plus phrase-presence checks across README, START_HERE, docs/README, and docs/first-task-guide
